Given.model_text:
Two simultaneous linear equations:
2x + y = 7 ... (1)
x - y = 2 (2)
We need to find the values of x and y.

Step1.student_text:
Add both equations:
2x + y + x - y = 7 + 2
3x = 9 so x = 3
Step1.model_text:
Add equation (1) and equation (2) to eliminate y:
(2x + y) + (x - y) = 7 + 2
3x = 9
x = 3

Step2.student_text:
Putting x = 3 in x - y = 2:
3 - y = 2
y = 1
Step2.model_text:
Substitute x = 3 into equation (2):
3 - y = 2
y = 3-2
y = 1

Step3.student_text:
Checking in equation 1: 2(3) + 1 = 7 correct.
(Did not check equation 2 separately)
Step3.model_text:
Verification — substitute x = 3, y = 1 in both equations:
Eq (1): 2(3) + 1 = 6 + 1 = 7 [correct]
Eq (2): 3 - 1 = 2 [correct]
Both equations are satisfied, so the solution is correct.

Conclusion.model_text:
The solution of the system of equations is:
x = 3 and y = 1

Two simultaneous linear equations:
2x + y = 7 ... (1)

model_answer.Step1:

3x = 9

model_answer.Step2:
Substitute x = 3 into equation (2):
3 - y = 2
y = 3-2
y = 1
model_answer.Step3:

Eq (1): 2(3) + 1 = 6 + 1 = 7 [correct]
Eq (2): 3 - 1 = 2 [correct]
Both equations are satisfied, so the solution is correct.
model_answer.Conclusion:
